title: Search Engines 101
main_text: A search engine answers a query by building an inverted index that maps every term to the documents containing it. Python is a common language for prototyping search algorithms because its standard library already ships a capable HTML parser.
author: Jane Doe

title: Writing A Tokenizer In Python
main_text: A tokenizer splits raw text into terms before they enter the inverted index. In python, Unicode normalization and casefold are essential so that accented and differently-cased variants of the same word land on the same term.
author: John Smith

title: Postings And Positions
main_text: Positional postings record not just that a term occurs in a document but exactly where. This is what makes phrase search possible later in a search pipeline, well beyond a plain inverted index of term presence.
author: Jane Doe
